text: The surgeon performs transanal submucosal dissection on a large adenoma located in the lower rectum using the Thunderbit tissue management system. The adenoma, approximately four centimeters in size, is visible from the dentin line to the lower rectum.

The surgeon performs submucosal dissection using a traditional trans-anal approach with Thunderbit assistance. An anal retractor reveals the tumor margin, and normal saline with 2% lidocaine is injected for submucosal elevation. A scalpel marks the lowest dissection margin from the dentin line, followed by Thunderbit for submucosal dissection. The dissection plane is clearly visible, with safe hemostasis throughout. The anal sphincter muscle is visible, and dissection extends to the lower rectum, with a clear dissection plan under this approach.
The surgeon ligates the pedicle of the tumor with a clamp and uses the Thunderbit device to transect it. The stump is then suture ligated using 3-0 Vicryl.

The whole procedure was performed smoothly within thirty minutes with minimal blood loss. The patient was discharged one day after the surgery. The final pathology proved cecil serrated adenoma with free surgical margin.
